rag_rules: Repair validator diagnostics by adding missing annex blocks or fixing syntax errors
Use only visible identifiers in AGREE contracts
Place component type contracts in component type annexes
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes
Use AGREE logical operators for branching logic and implications
generic_rules: Repair only the diagnosed issue without changing unrelated architecture
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations
Use nested if-then-else for branching logic in AGREE contracts
Use AGREE logical operators such as and, or, not, and => for implications
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside annex agree {** ... **}; blocks
Component type annexes are the normal location for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Component implementation annexes are used for implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not use unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations in AGREE contracts
Do not use AGREE scalar type names like integer or boolean; use int, real, and bool instead
